channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Plasmonic Dirac-vortex lasers via three-dimensional photonic mass vortices engineering
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70833-1

The intensity profiles and polarization states bestowed upon a beam by topological photonic crystals are limited to the spatial symmetries that can be realized with the crystal lattice. Here, authors demonstrate plasmonic Dirac-vortex lasing with tailorable intensity and polarisation distributions, based on photonic mass vortices engineering.
item.dc:title: Plasmonic Dirac-vortex lasers via three-dimensional photonic mass vortices engineering
item.dc:creator: Mou Zhong
Xiaoqiong Bi
Mengyuan Song
Nanli Mou
Delin Zhang
Xiaolu Zhuo
Jingtian Hu
Biye Xie
Xianyu Ao
Jun Guan

item.title: Epsilon-near-zero time-gate for high-fidelity spatial information transfer through dynamic scattering media
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71039-1

Here, authors demonstrate an ultrafast time-gating protocol using a sub-micron-thick layer of epsilon-near-zero indium-tin oxide, yielding a strong four-wave mixing signal while reducing scattering and scintillation.
item.dc:title: Epsilon-near-zero time-gate for high-fidelity spatial information transfer through dynamic scattering media
item.dc:creator: Yang Xu
Saumya Choudhary
Long D. Nguyen
Matthew Klein
Shivashankar Vangala
J. Keith Miller
Eric G. Johnson
Joshua R. Hendrickson
M. Zahirul Alam
Robert W. Boyd

item.title: Deciphering the liquid-solid interactions in dealkalization of O3 layered oxides
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70581-2

High-performance battery materials often degrade during standard cleaning processes. Here, the authors identify why water washing causes structural collapse in sodium-based oxides and demonstrate that ethylene glycol can safely remove impurities while maintaining the material’s integrity.
item.dc:title: Deciphering the liquid-solid interactions in dealkalization of O3 layered oxides
item.dc:creator: Wujiu Zhang
Jifu Zhu
Amin Song
Mengjie Huang
Xin Zhang
Xiekun Xu
Chenyang Liu
Chuhan Yuan
Nan Li
Shipeng Zhang
Yiran Ying
Lei Dong
Chao Shen
Keyu Xie

item.title: A bioinspired microdevice unifying energy storage and actuation through hydration control
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 19 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70831-3

Conjugated polymers are promising for compact energy storage and actuation devices though it is challenging to balance these properties. Here the authors report a polymer system suppressing ion hydration for a multifunctional microdevice.
item.dc:title: A bioinspired microdevice unifying energy storage and actuation through hydration control
item.dc:creator: Wenlan Zhang
Leandro Merces
Jiachen Ma
Christian Becker
